whenIAmNeeded.title: I'm needed when:
items: - Repair is more expensive than downtime: budget for standard solutions exceeds common sense
- Problem-bumerang: You're repairing the same node for the third time in a year
- Deadlock compromise: A choice is required between 'fast', 'high quality', and 'cheap'
- Dead zone: specialists say 'everything is fine', but there's no result
- Path to the unknown: you need to create something that doesn't have an instruction yet
- Modernization without procurement: expanding the capabilities of existing equipment or adding new functions
- Adaptability: adapting systems to new, unplanned working conditions
whenIAmNeeded.footer: My method: stop the pressure and think differently.

whatIDo.title: What I Do
items: Design system architecture with a focus on long-term reliability

philosophy.title: My Philosophy
description: System failure, like any disaster, is caused by a coincidence of several factors.
The vast majority of failures result from not considering the system as a whole,
either during the design or development stages.

projects.title: Interesting Cases
info: Selected technical cases will appear here shortly.
In the meantime, feel free to contact me to discuss an idea or a problem.

contacts.title: Contacts
description: If you are facing a technical problem that is hard to structure or fix — write to me.
I am always open to complex tasks and interesting proposals.
